commit.message:
SBM-005: Add server event logging

Enhanced server logging when events are relayed:

New logging format for events:
  [RELAY] {player_name} rolled {dice_expr} → {total} ({intent})

Example logs:
  INFO:server.handlers:[RELAY] Alice rolled d20 → 15 (attack)
  INFO:server.handlers:[RELAY] Bob rolled 2d6+1 → 9 (dodge)
  INFO:server.handlers:[RELAY] Charlie rolled 3d8 → 17 (magic)

Event details extracted:
- Player name
- Dice expression (e.g. d20, 2d6+1, 3d8)
- Total result
- Intent (optional, e.g. attack, dodge)

Benefits:
- Clear visibility of game activity
- Track all rolls happening on server
- Help with debugging
- Monitor server relay performance
- Useful for game logging/auditing

Implementation:
- Extract event data in _relay_message()
- Format player roll with all relevant details
- Log at INFO level for visibility
- Kept debug logs for relay details

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

SBM-004: Implement command history in client

Features:
- Command history tracking (all rolls)
- Navigate with UP/DOWN arrow keys
- History persists during session
- Clear input when reaching end of history
- Cursor positioned at end of recalled command

Implementation:
- command_history: list[str] for storing commands
- history_index: tracking position in history
- action_history_prev(): UP arrow handling
- action_history_next(): DOWN arrow handling
- Reset history_index on new input
- Help text updated with shortcuts

Usage:
- /r d20 attack
- UP arrow → recalls previous command
- UP again → goes further back
- DOWN arrow → goes forward
- DOWN at end → clears input

matching the pattern already implemented in the sync client. Clear stale

commit.message:
fix: CI pipeline — pnpm version conflict, build scripts, turbo config

Three issues fixed:

1. pnpm version conflict: pnpm/action-setup@v4 errored because both
   the workflow (version: 10) and package.json (packageManager: pnpm@10.33.0)
   specified versions. Fixed by removing the version from the workflow —
   package.json is the source of truth.

2. API build script tried to execute the server (tsc && node dist/index.js).
   Changed to just 'tsc' — running the server is for dev/deploy, not build.

3. Turbo typecheck/test tasks depended on ^build, causing unnecessary cascade
   builds that failed. Removed the dependency — typecheck and test run independently.

Also:
- Added 'Generate route tree' step in CI (vite build for TanStack Router)
- Fixed web typecheck script (removed shell redirect incompatible with CI)
- Removed unused rollup-plugin-visualizer dependency

Fix crash: reuse original compressed chunks where data is unchanged

The game's statically-linked Oodle may be a different version than the
oo2core_7_win64.dll we use for compression, causing crashes when loading
saves that were fully recompressed.

New strategy: only recompress chunks whose decompressed content actually
changed; all other chunks keep their original compressed bytes verbatim.

- oodle.parse_compressed_chunks(body_bytes) — extract raw compressed
  bytes per chunk without decompressing (used during import)
- oodle.recompress() — new orig_compressed + orig_raw params; compares
  each chunk slice dynamically and reuses original if identical
- save_converter: passes orig_raw snapshot and orig_compressed to
  recompress so no-op chunks are written byte-for-byte from the source
- Result: unedited roundtrip is byte-identical to original; a typical
  scalar edit (LoreLevel etc.) recompresses only 1 of 23 chunks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix crash: preserve original chunk boundaries on recompress

The game crashed (EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION) when loading imports
because recompress() was producing a single 374 MB chunk instead of
matching the original 23-chunk structure.

Changes:
- decompress() now returns (bytes, list[int]) — raw data plus per-chunk
  uncompressed sizes
- recompress() accepts chunk_sizes and splits the output accordingly;
  falls back to 10 MB chunks when not provided
- save_converter.py threads chunk_sizes from export JSON through import
- oodle_compress.c: fix output buffer too small for large chunks
  (src_len + 65536 → src_len + src_len/128 + 262144), allowing the DLL
  to compress chunks up to ~155 MB without returning failure

commit.message:
fix: suppress banner for Cobra internal __complete commands

PersistentPreRun previously only skipped ui.Header() for the user-visible
'completion' subcommand. Cobra also dispatches internal '__complete' and
'__completeNoDesc' commands when shell scripts request live completions
(e.g. pressing TAB in fish). Those calls also write to stdout, so the banner
would appear inside the completion response, corrupting fish's display.

Extend the guard to also skip PersistentPreRun when cmd.Name() is
'__complete' or '__completeNoDesc'.
